title: viola johnstonii
intro: Viola johnstonii, also known as the Johnston's pansy, is a species of flowering plant in the violet family (Violaceae) native to the South Island of New Zealand. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. It is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and supporting immune function. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions.
